title: Small Kitchen Appliances - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Small Kitchen Appliances 13 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CASCADE AIDS PROJECT TOD'S CORNER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household goods (including kitchen items), bedding and linens, and other items. Accepts donations of men's clothing, kitchenware, small appliances, bedding and linens, and bathroom supplies. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE GRESHAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 464 NE 219th Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE PORTLAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 3969 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE TIGARD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 9806 SW Tigard Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER ITEMS EXCHANGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people donating furniture and appliances to those needing those items. Small items like blankets and hygiene items can be brought to the Family Resource Center. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK LINC MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides household goods, budgeting, job, and life skills mentoring, and connections to local food banks and community meals. May also assist with rent, electric, and propane vouchers when available. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed. Utility assistance is available once per year. 278 SE 8th Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (541) 475-4446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF BENTON COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, personal hygiene items, linens (bedding, towels, etc.), baby bundles (newborn baby supplies and clothing), kitchen supplies, and a gift closet (for when community members can't afford to purchase gifts for special occasions). 4900 NW Hwy 99 Suite 102 Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Low-income community members in need Hours: Helpline: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-1:30pm Email (541) 757-8111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARLA'S MALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift store with books, toys, household items, new hygiene products, knick-knacks, and clothing including interview or work-appropriate attire. Offers volunteer work experience and credit recovery for students working at the store. Also accepts donations of new and gently used items. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Thrift Store: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 8am-11am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: June 16th-July 17th Monday-Thursday 8:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM ALLIANCE CHURCH FURNITURE BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gently-used furniture, mattresses, and household goods to people who have received permanent housing and need assistance transitioning from their shelter into a home. 555 Gaines Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 581-2129 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes, clothing for adults, household items (kitchenware and small appliances), prescription assistance, gas money (for job interviews or appointments), emergency motel vouchers, and ID assistance. 51484 Morson Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-noon and 12:30pm-2pm Email (541) 536-6135 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LEBANON CONFERENCE FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a food pantry, providing a 3 day supply of food. Also has a rotating supply of clothing, shoes, furniture, appliances, and other household goods supplied by donations. Food, clothing items, and household items accepted for donation. 100 Main Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Wednesday 9am-11am and 11:30am-3pm Email (541) 258-5405 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY LINDHOLM SOCIAL SERVICE OFFICE EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household items, small appliances, furniture, beds, propane vouchers for heating, emergency prescription assistance, and bus tokens when available. Assistance depends on the zip code. 456 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing and shoes including interview-ready clothes, food, home goods, kitchenware (pots, pans, dish sets), and hygiene items. Workforce closet available with work boots and shoes, rain and safety gear. Shopping allowed once per month. Exceptions made for special circumstances. Accepts donations of food, clothing, bedding, linens, kitchenware, toys and games, and hygiene supplies. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Thrift Store: Monday/Wednesday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-noon and 1pm-3pm, 1st Saturday of the month noon-3pm -Donations: Visit website for current hours Email (541) 753-1420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A smaller version of Vina's Free Thrift Store with clothes, hygiene items, household goods, shoes, and more. Items vary by donation and are not guaranteed. Services are offered at two locations in collaboration with Alsea School District and South Benton Resource Center. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Must reside in area of closet locations and meet their eligibility criteria Hours: Monroe: Tuesday 10am-noon, South Benton Resource Center, 648 Orchard Street, Monroe, OR 97456 Alsea: By appointment with Alsea School District staff. Location will be provided when appointment is confirmed.
